summary: Demand evidence agents completed, but readiness still has blockers.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema behavior must verify harness boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents before PRD readiness.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
risks: If full-tool agents mutate project files without blocker, demand communication can corrupt target projects.

cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit to detect and block target-project mutations by full-tool Claude agents.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot function captures SHA256 digests of all project files except allowed artifact roots
evidence.why: Core boundary audit mechanism for before/after comparison

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after snapshots to detect added, modified, or deleted files
evidence.why: Mutation detection logic that identifies boundary violations

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshot captured before agent execution: buildBoundarySnapshot(plan.project_root, [plan.output_dir])
evidence.why: Establishes baseline for detecting mutations during evidence gathering

evidence.summary: Post-execution boundary audit compares snapshots and records changes in result.boundary field
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement of boundary through actual comparison

evidence.summary: BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers created for any detected changes outside allowed artifact roots
evidence.why: Enforcement mechanism that blocks PRD readiness on mutations

evidence.summary: execution_policy boundary configuration declares project_mutation forbidden and lists allowed_write_roots
evidence.why: Policy declaration that evidence agents cannot modify target project files

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt hard-codes boundary rules into agent instructions: Boundary: do not modify target project files
evidence.why: Instruction-level boundary enforcement communicated to evidence agents

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS define explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with writes_code: false
evidence.why: Protocol-level contract that evidence gathering is read-only

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness validates that required evidence agents (explorer, cross-checker, verifier) have completed
evidence.why: PRD readiness gate requiring full evidence chain before proceeding

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation constructs claude CLI args with permission-mode, allowedTools, and no-session-persistence flags
evidence.why: Provider-level tool and permission configuration for agent execution

risks: If boundary snapshot logic fails or has whitelist bugs, mutations could go undetected
If evidence agents ignore instructions or tools bypass boundary, target project could be corrupted before detection
missing: Integration test evidence showing boundary audit blocking an actual mutation attempt
Evidence log or artifact demonstrating a BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker being created and processed

result.notes: Evidence dispatch code implements comprehensive boundary audit with before/after snapshots, policy enforcement, and blocker generation. Cross-check recommended to verify runtime behavior matches code claims and stress-test with actual mutation attempts.

agent_results.claim: Independent cross-check confirms YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements comprehensive boundary audit system with before/after SHA256 snapshots, policy enforcement, and blocker generation to detect and block target-project mutations by full-tool Claude agents.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot function iterates all project files, computes SHA256 digest via fileDigest (line 91), skips allowedRoots, returns Map of file to digest
evidence.why: Core baseline capture mechanism confirmed with exact implementation

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after Maps, detects deleted (missing in after), modified (different digest), and added (missing in before) files
evidence.why: Mutation detection algorithm confirmed with all three change types

evidence.summary: boundaryBefore snapshot captured before agent loop: buildBoundarySnapshot(plan.project_root, [plan.output_dir])
evidence.why: Baseline timing confirmed before any evidence agent execution

evidence.summary: boundaryAfter captured after agent loop, diffed against boundaryBefore, result.boundary populated with project_mutation status and changes array
evidence.why: Post-execution comparison and recording confirmed

evidence.summary: boundaryBlockers mapped from changes with BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION code, merged into runtimeBlockers, finalReadiness.prd_ready set to false when blockers present
evidence.why: Blocker generation and PRD readiness enforcement confirmed

evidence.summary: execution_policy boundary section declares project_mutation forbidden and allowed_write_roots array with output_dir
evidence.why: Policy configuration confirms boundary contract at dispatch level

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt hard-codes boundary rule at line 337: 'Boundary: do not modify target project files. If verification would require mutation, report it in missing.'
evidence.why: Instruction-level boundary enforcement confirmed in agent prompts

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross_checker, and verifier with writes_code: false on lines 59, 66, and 74
evidence.why: Protocol-level read-only contract confirmed for all evidence agent roles

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness iterates requiredRoles, pushes EVIDENCE_AGENT_RESULT_REQUIRED blocker if any role lacks complete result
evidence.why: PRD readiness gate confirmed requiring full evidence chain

evidence.summary: Agent prompt includes explicit allowed artifact root and boundary restriction: 'Allowed artifact/scratch root for this dispatch: {plan.output_dir}'
evidence.why: Boundary rules communicate write scope explicitly to evidence agents

evidence.summary: For toolProfile === 'full', prompt states: 'Full tools may be available for stress probes, but this demand evidence protocol still forbids modifying target project files.'
evidence.why: Full-tool profile boundary restriction confirmed despite tool availability

evidence.summary: crossCheckRequested logic detects any agent result recommending cross_check, then expands requiredRoles to include explorer, cross-checker, and verifier
evidence.why: Cross-check policy escalation confirmed requiring full triple-agent verification

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation constructs claude CLI with -p flag, permission-mode, allowedTools, disallowedTools, and no-session-persistence flag
evidence.why: Provider-level tool and permission control confirmed for agent execution environment

assumptions: SHA256 hash function in Node.js crypto createHash works correctly for file content comparison
Git file discovery and filesystem walk cover all relevant project files
Evidence agents receive and process the boundary rules in their prompts
Claude CLI permission-mode and tool flags actually restrict tool availability during agent execution
risks: If allowedRoots whitelist logic bugs, mutations to sensitive paths could be excluded from snapshot comparison
If buildBoundarySnapshot misses files due to git ignore or filesystem walk bugs, mutations could go undetected
If evidence agents ignore prompt instructions or find tool bypass, target project could be mutated before boundary audit runs
If provider-adapter tool configuration is incorrect, agents may have write tools despite boundary policy
missing: Integration test demonstrating BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker creation after actual file mutation attempt
Test coverage or evidence log showing boundary audit detecting and reporting specific change types (added, modified, deleted)
Evidence that provider-adapter tool flags actually prevent Write/Edit tool usage in spawned Claude agents
Fixture or artifact from a successful boundary enforcement scenario (blocker created, PRD readiness blocked)

result.notes: All nine explorer claims verified through independent code reads. Boundary audit implementation confirmed: SHA256 snapshots capture project state, diff detects all change types, blockers prevent PRD readiness, policy and prompts declare boundary, protocols set writes_code false, PRD gate requires full evidence chain, provider configures tools and permissions. Missing integration test evidence and runtime tool restriction verification remain as gaps between code claims and proven runtime behavior.

agent_results.claim: Verification confirms boundary audit implementation exists in code, but critical gaps remain between code claims and proven runtime behavior. Assumptions are properly separated from facts, evidence paths are valid, and missing data is correctly identified as blockers to PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot and diffBoundarySnapshots functions provide code-level boundary audit mechanism
evidence.why: Code structure confirms implementation intent, not runtime behavior

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshot before/after logic and blocker generation code paths exist
evidence.why: Execution flow confirms design, not stress-tested reliability

evidence.summary: Protocol definitions and PRD readiness gate code enforce evidence agent requirements
evidence.why: Policy enforcement confirmed at code level only

evidence.summary: Cross-checker correctly identified 4 runtime behavior assumptions as separate from facts
evidence.why: Assumption-fact separation verified per verifier protocol

evidence.summary: Both agents identified same missing evidence: integration tests, runtime tool restriction proof, enforcement artifacts
evidence.why: Missing data consistently reflected across evidence chain

risks: Code claims about boundary enforcement may not hold under real agent execution with full tools available
Claude CLI tool restrictions may not actually prevent Write/Edit tool usage in spawned agents
Evidence agents may process boundary prompts incorrectly or find bypass mechanisms
Missing integration tests mean boundary violations could occur undetected in production
missing: Integration test or stress test demonstrating boundary audit blocking actual mutation attempt
Evidence log or artifact showing BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker created and processed
Verification that provider-adapter tool configuration actually prevents Write/Edit tools in spawned Claude processes
Test coverage for diffBoundarySnapshots detecting all three change types (added, modified, deleted)
Fixture or log from successful boundary enforcement scenario

result.notes: Verifier confirms evidence chain properly separates assumptions from facts, all evidence paths are valid and verifiable, and missing data is correctly identified as blockers to PRD readiness. The critical gap is between code-level implementation claims and proven runtime behavior. Missing integration test evidence means acceptance criteria 'Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation' is partially met: paths are cited, but boundary audit has not yet demonstrated runtime blocking capability. Proceed is appropriate because the missing evidence is correctly reflected in blockers, assumptions are not promoted to facts, and PRD readiness will remain blocked until runtime behavior is proven.

status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
